behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (CSV reader -> validator -> DLQ writer). The real failure is often in handoff of row metadata (row number) between modules, not just validator logic. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of validation can miss dropped/misaligned row numbers. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; an in-process DLQ sink and parser wiring is sufficient, so System is rejected. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model judgment or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline end-to-end in-process with a small CSV fixture containing missing required fields; assert each invalid row is emitted to the DLQ payload with the exact original CSV row number.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam (date parsing/normalization logic). MOCK-MISS: failure is algorithmic parsing/format handling inside one transform component. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. Rejection-gate: Integration is rejected because behavior text is only about conversion correctness across input formats, which can be fully exercised by direct function inputs/outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of the date normalization function with ISO 8601, US, and European examples (including ambiguous dates), asserting exact canonical output or explicit rejection for invalid/ambiguous cases.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (process memory, runtime scheduling, timeout behavior under load). REAL-INFRA: this failure depends on actual memory pressure and wall-clock execution; mocks hide it. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory fakes or tiny datasets often pass while real runs OOM/timeout. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real runtime configuration; assert completion before timeout and verify peak memory stays below the configured limit.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (ingest client + retry policy + batch controller). MOCK-MISS: unit tests of backoff math alone can miss controller behavior (continue batch vs fail). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: System considered because S3 is networked, but rejected since the behavior is explicitly about response-code handling and retry policy, which is reproducible with an in-process stubbed S3 client and controllable clock; no required real network semantics are stated. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire pipeline modules with a stub S3 client that returns 503 for initial attempts then success; assert retry count, exponential delay schedule, and that batch processing continues to completion.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam with persistence boundary (checkpoint/idempotency logic + DB writes). The real failure is interaction between write semantics and restart recovery state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: System considered due restart wording, but rejected because restart can be simulated by stopping/recreating pipeline components against the same local PostgreSQL state; no required OS-level crash/subprocess semantics are specified. LLM/MULTI-STEP not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real local PostgreSQL test DB, process part of a batch, simulate restart by re-instantiating pipeline with persisted checkpoint state, resume processing, and assert no duplicate rows (by PK/unique key and row counts).
